text: The surgeon performs pelvic nodal dissection following the medial to lateral mobilization of the rectal tube, using robotic instruments to carefully separate and remove lymph nodes from the pelvic region.
The surgeon extends the lateral peritoneal cuts over the external iliac vessels, enhancing exposure for the pelvic nodal dissection.
The surgeon identifies and fully dissects the external iliac vessels, which form the lateral margin of the surgical field.
The surgeon mobilizes the ureter medially to facilitate nodal clearance during the robotic lateral pelvic lymph node dissection.
The surgeon creates a paravesical space by dissecting between the obturator internus muscle and the internal iliac vasculature, aiming to access the nodal zone.
The surgeon traces the obliterated umbilical artery downward, visually identifying and reaching the internal iliac artery within the surgical field.
The surgeon reflects the fibrofatty tissue containing lymph nodes medially away from the external iliac vessels, revealing the underlying anatomical structures.
The surgeon carefully reflects the nodal tissue away from the obturator internus muscle, revealing the underlying anatomical structures and maintaining clear separation from the muscle.
The surgeon separates the nodal tissue from the bladder fat pad and dissects it away from the confluence of the external and internal iliac vessels, revealing the anatomical structures beneath.
The surgeon carefully dissects the nodal tissue surrounding the obturator nerve, completing the clearance of the nodal zone between the obturator internus muscle and the internal iliac vasculature.
The surgeon identifies the second nodal zone located between the internal iliac vasculature and the hypogastric plexus, preparing for further dissection in this area.
The surgeon gently opens the area surrounding the hypogastric plexus, ensuring preservation of this nerve structure while continuing dissection.
The surgeon carefully dissects the node-bearing fatty tissue away from the branches of the internal iliac artery, ensuring clear separation from the vascular structures.
The surgeon has dissected below the recommended lower limit of the inferior vesicle artery, revealing the anatomical structures in this area.
